generated_from: Fextralife Maps table fetched 2026-07-05; PoE2DB Waystones page checked 2026-07-05 for endgame map and unique-map context; HC tips are curated from listed biome, boss, tileset, notes, and special mechanics.

maps.name: Alpine Ridge
maps.boss: Gelida, the Frost-Tongue & Ignatia, the Flame-Sworn
maps.notes: This is a Tower map. Completing it reveals a portion of the Atlas and allows you to use Tablets to empower nearby maps.
maps.summary: A Mountain biome tower map used to reveal Atlas territory and enable Tablet empowerment for nearby maps.
threats: Boss encounter: Gelida, the Frost-Tongue & Ignatia, the Flame-Sworn.
Tower value comes from Atlas reveal; do not over-roll it just for rewards.
Cave or mountain tiles can hide threats around corners and choke points.
tips: Treat the dual Gelida/Ignatia fight as alternating cold and fire pressure; keep both resistances capped before spending a good tower node.
Use the tower for Atlas reveal first, then place rewarding Tablets on safer surrounding maps rather than forcing hard mods here.
Mountain corners can hide packs close to the entrance of a room, so step into new sections slowly instead of blinking deep.

maps.name: Ancient Gateway
maps.summary: A Atlas map with limited public notes; treat its layout and boss state as partially unknown.
threats: Boss data is missing or unconfirmed, so assume unknown burst windows.
tips: Because public boss and layout data is thin, run the first copy as a scouting map with only mods your character can ignore.
Keep a portal-side mental note of the route you used; unknown gateway-style maps are worse when retreat paths are improvised.
Skip reduced recovery plus monster damage stacks until the boss state is confirmed for your build.

maps.name: Augury
maps.notes: Features three Stone Altars that must be activated to reach the central area. The boss only appears if the map has two or more rare affixes or a specific boss modifier.
maps.summary: A Grass, Forest, Swamp biome, based on Jiquani's Machinarium map. Features three Stone Altars that must be activated to reach the central area. The boss only appears if the map has two or more rare affixes or a specific boss modifier.
threats: Boss encounter: Gressor-Kul, the Apex.
Wet terrain and islands can limit clean retreat paths.
tips: Activate the three Stone Altars only after nearby packs are dead, so altar spawns do not mix with uncleared rares.
If you are trying to avoid the boss on HC, do not accidentally make the map rare with two or more rare affixes.
Before entering the central area, refresh flasks and cooldowns; the altar route can bait you into starting the boss check tired.

maps.name: Azmerian Ranges
maps.boss: The King in the Mists & The Brambleghast
maps.notes: This area is teeming with multiple Azmerian wisps, guiding players through the treacherous terrain.
maps.summary: A Mountain biome map. This area is teeming with multiple Azmerian wisps, guiding players through the treacherous terrain.
threats: Boss encounter: The King in the Mists & The Brambleghast.
Multiple bosses or repeated boss waves can overlap abilities and exhaust flask/recovery windows.
Cave or mountain tiles can hide threats around corners and choke points.
tips: Follow Azmerian wisps at your own pace; do not chase them through uncleared mountain bends on a HC character.
Plan for King in the Mists control effects and Brambleghast area denial in the same run, so avoid recovery and movement penalties.
If both bosses pressure the same space, kite back through cleared ground rather than trying to finish one during overlap.

maps.name: Backwash

maps.summary: A Forest, Swamp biome, based on Jungle Ruins map. A dense forest map culminating in a fight against the repulsive Yaota.
threats: Boss encounter: Yaota, the Loathsome.
Wet terrain and islands can limit clean retreat paths.
tips: Give Yaota room and avoid standing in lingering swamp effects while trying to finish the boss quickly.

In Backwash's forest sections, clear edges first because trees can hide monsters and ground effects near the center.

threats: Boss encounter: Veynar, the Frostbane.
tips: Treat Veynar as a cold burst check; bring freeze/chill answers and do not add reduced recovery on first clears.

maps.summary: A Forest biome map. Characterized by a linear layout with tight, narrow passages that restrict movement.

Constrained paths can body-block HC characters and make ground effects harder to dodge.
Damage, speed, and extra projectile modifiers can turn this into a skip for undergeared HC builds.
tips: The narrow linear route is dangerous with fast rares; pull packs backward until you have room to dodge Gorian-style slams.
Do not add extra projectiles or monster speed while learning this map, because the corridors remove many sidestep options.

Between waves, reset position deliberately instead of accepting the next spawn wherever the last boss died.

maps.name: The Silent Cave
maps.notes: This map contains many Rare monsters trapped in Essences and an imprisoned boss that absorbs Essences from them. Requires a Waystone of Tier 11 or higher to access.
maps.summary: A Desert biome map. This map contains many Rare monsters trapped in Essences and an imprisoned boss that absorbs Essences from them. Requires a Waystone of Tier 11 or higher to access.
threats: Boss encounter: Random map boss.
Rare-heavy mechanics can stack dangerous monster modifiers faster than a normal map.
Cave or mountain tiles can hide threats around corners and choke points.
tips: Essence rares upgrade the imprisoned boss, so choose which crystals to release rather than opening everything by habit.
Kill freed rares in cleared cave space before touching another essence; stacked rare modifiers are the real HC danger.
If the boss already has scary modifiers, leave optional essences alone and take the completion.

maps.name: The Stone Citadel
maps.notes: A key endgame encounter where players face an empowered version of the Act 3 boss. This area is only accessible with a Tier 15 or above Waystone.
maps.summary: A pinnacle-style Vaal City biome, based on Jiquani's Machinarium, The Black Chambers citadel encounter with a major boss and high-tier Waystone requirements.
threats: Boss encounter: Doryani, Royal Thaumaturge.
Citadel bosses are high-tier progression fights with low tolerance for mistakes.
Damage, speed, and extra projectile modifiers can turn this into a skip for undergeared HC builds.
tips: Doryani is a major progression boss; treat the map like a bossing session with capped elemental defenses and a practiced movement plan.
The Vaal machinery tileset can hide hazards under spell effects, so lower visual clutter and avoid greed during beam or area-denial patterns.
Do not combine this attempt with risky tablet or waystone pressure on HC.

maps.name: The Viridian Wildwood
maps.notes: This area is shrouded in darkness and contains Omen altars. Players can find several chests that contain powerful Omens.
maps.summary: A Forest, Swamp biome, based on The Grelwood, The Viridian Wildwood map. This area is shrouded in darkness and contains Omen altars. Players can find several chests that contain powerful Omens.
threats: Boss data is missing or unconfirmed, so assume unknown burst windows.
Wet terrain and islands can limit clean retreat paths.
Damage, speed, and extra projectile modifiers can turn this into a skip for undergeared HC builds.
tips: Darkness changes the map's pacing; follow visibility and Omen altars carefully instead of sprinting toward rewards.
Several powerful bosses can be found here, so assume any detour can become a serious fight and keep flasks full.
On HC, skip altar paths that pull you through uncleared darkness while carrying dangerous map modifiers.

maps.name: Untainted Paradise
maps.notes: A unique map offering a 200-400% increased Experience gain bonus. However, monsters within this paradise do not drop any items.
maps.summary: A Water biome map. A unique map offering a 200-400% increased Experience gain bonus. However, monsters within this paradise do not drop any items.
threats: Boss data is missing or unconfirmed, so assume unknown burst windows.
Wet terrain and islands can limit clean retreat paths.
tips: The experience bonus is tempting, but the monster level penalty can make overconfidence lethal; run it only when your defenses outclass the tier.
Do not add dangerous modifiers just because the map is for XP; dying deletes the entire point of the run.
Clear slowly enough to preserve flask charges, especially if your build depends on recovery on kill.

maps.name: Vaal City
maps.notes: This map will only appear on the Atlas near a Stone Citadel.
maps.summary: A pinnacle-style Vaal City biome, based on Utzaal citadel encounter with a major boss and high-tier Waystone requirements.
threats: Boss encounter: Viper Napuatzi.
Citadel bosses are high-tier progression fights with low tolerance for mistakes.
Damage, speed, and extra projectile modifiers can turn this into a skip for undergeared HC builds.
tips: Treat Vaal City as progression content, not routine mapping; bring a boss-ready setup and skip risky map pressure.
Use Stone Citadel region maps to set up Doryani attempts; avoid exhausting your character on risky rolls first.
Keep poison/chaos mitigation in mind for Viper Napuatzi and do not fight in cramped Vaal corners.

maps.name: Vaal Foundry
maps.notes: A hazardous, molten-filled foundry culminating in a battle with a fiery elemental.
maps.summary: A Mountain, Vaal City biome, based on The Molten Vault map. A hazardous, molten-filled foundry culminating in a battle with a fiery elemental.
threats: Boss encounter: Gulzal, the Living Furnace.
Cave or mountain tiles can hide threats around corners and choke points.
tips: For Vaal Foundry, overcap fire mitigation for Gulzal and avoid lava or furnace ground effects even when the boss is low.
On Vaal Foundry's mountain terrain, scout bends before spending mobility skills because burst threats can wait around corners.
In Vaal Foundry's Vaal tiles, lower your pace and avoid fighting rares on top of machinery effects.

maps.name: Vaults of Kamasa
maps.notes: A rewarding map with 100-200% increased item rarity. All chests are Magic or Rare, but the area contains no monsters. Only spawns near a Stone Citadel.
maps.summary: A pinnacle-style Vaal City biome, based on Jiquani's Sanctum citadel encounter with a major boss and high-tier Waystone requirements.
threats: No listed boss, but rare monsters and map modifiers can still create lethal spikes.
Citadel bosses are high-tier progression fights with low tolerance for mistakes.
Cave or mountain tiles can hide threats around corners and choke points.
tips: Rare and magic chests are the reward, so clear around each chest before opening it and avoid chain-opening under pressure.
Rarity value is already built in; HC characters should prioritize safe chest access over extra damage mods.
If the Vaal City route gets cramped, pull monsters away from chest clusters before interacting.

maps.name: Wayward Isle
maps.summary: A Atlas map culminating in a fight against Lord of the Pit.
threats: Boss encounter: Lord of the Pit.
tips: For Wayward Isle, treat Lord of the Pit as a heavy physical/fire check and skip damage stacking until the pattern is familiar.

Before Wayward Isle's final section, refill flasks, wait for cooldowns, and keep a known escape route behind you.

maps.name: Wetlands
maps.notes: An expansive open area layout where players must face two formidable earth elementals.
maps.summary: A Swamp biome map. An expansive open area layout where players must face two formidable earth elementals.
threats: Boss encounter: 2x Gorian, the Moving Earth.
Open sightlines can layer projectiles or fast rare pressure from several angles.
Multiple bosses or repeated boss waves can overlap abilities and exhaust flask/recovery windows.
Wet terrain and islands can limit clean retreat paths.
tips: Two Gorian bosses make this a positioning check; separate them with terrain and do not tunnel one while the other winds up.
The open swamp gives room, so use it: kite in broad circles through cleared ground rather than standing between both bosses.
Avoid extra area damage or monster speed until your build can survive overlapping Moving Earth attacks.
